steps.title: Recognize two independent clauses joined by 'but'
steps.content: ”This bat-and-ball game was derived from cricket” ends one complete thought. “Kilikiti differs from cricket in a few key ways” starts another. The word “but” between them signals a contrast.

steps.title: Apply the comma-before-conjunction rule
steps.content:
A comma before a coordinating conjunction joins two independent clauses.

Here, that's “cricket, but kilikiti differs...” The other options either position the comma after “but” (wrong position) or use a colon (incorrect for coordinating conjunctions).

That confirms C is correct.
